title: SACRAMENTO, CA – This week, Assemblymember Connolly (D-San Rafael) successfully passed four bills out of their respective policy committees in the State Assembly. This includes:
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: AB 846 will provide a clear process to accelerate and streamline wildfire preparedness and vegetation management.
AB 846 will help communities respond faster to wildfire risks while protecting vulnerable ecosystems.
AB 419 requires schools to post the 'Immigration-Enforcement Actions at California Schools Guide for Students and Families' in schools and on their website.
AB 419 ensures that immigrant students and families know their rights regarding school enrollment and information protection.
AB 674 helps lower-income consumers replace older, high-polluting vehicles with cleaner transportation.
AB 674 will reduce pollution in communities most affected by high vehicle emissions.
AB 1140 authorizes a pilot program for safety improvements in prisons by utilizing single-occupancy cells.
AB 1140 aims to better protect correctional employees and inmates.
